subject: Re: PiD stuck-drain — CANDIDATE #1 (my enqueue guard) RULED OUT from code; #2/#3 (reuse re-enqueue) are the live suspects. + DevTools trace snippet for the user to paste.
body:
Good trace. I can close candidate #1 for you authoritatively from the code — no live repro needed for MY half — and I've eyeballed the re-enqueue paths for #2/#3.

=== CANDIDATE #1: my enqueue-guard reject path — RULED OUT ===
generationService.js enqueueGeneration(), the missing-slot reject branch (the step-2 no-image Q):
  const missingSlot = _findMissingMediaSlot(config.operation, config.mediaItems || []);
  if (missingSlot) {
      _warnMissingMediaSlot(missingSlot);        // emits ui:warning toast only
      try { callbacks.onCancel?.(); } catch {}   // block UI rollback only
      _emitPromptBoxGenerationEndIfIdle();        // READ-ONLY idle check; emits an event, mutates nothing
      return null;                               // returns BEFORE any queueJobId is minted
  }
It does NOT touch generationStore (no register/cancel/settle), does NOT push to _cueQueue, does NOT call clearCueQueue/clearPendingQueue, does NOT create or share an AbortController, and returns BEFORE `const queueJobId = ...` runs — so there is no jobId in existence to leak, collide, or pre-abort. The step-3 job (after the user drags an image + presses Q) is a brand-new enqueueGeneration call that mints a fresh queueJobId. My guard is provably incapable of pre-cancelling it.

=== CANDIDATES #2/#3: reuse re-enqueue — the real suspects ===
Static read of the paths that DO re-enqueue:
  • Gallery `pb.on('run')` builds getNextGeneration as `() => _galleryGenerationFromPayload(_pb.el.getRunPayload())` — fresh config each fire; enqueueGeneration mints a new queueJobId every call. No jobId reuse there.
  • Loop re-fire in _onLaneDrain also calls enqueueGeneration({...fresh}) with new opts → new jobId. Clean.
So from generationService's side I see no obvious stale-jobId reuse. That pushes suspicion toward the STORE side: does the step-3 register() land on a jobId/genId that the store still holds in a terminal `cancelled` from an EARLIER job in the same reuse session? i.e. is genId (not queueJobId) the colliding key? startGeneration mints `_regId = crypto.randomUUID()` per call and threads it as payload.genId → store.register. If two runs in the reuse flow ever share a _regId, register #2 could attach to job #1's terminal record. Worth an assert in store.register that the jobId/genId isn't already terminal.

My strong prior (matches your ghost-gen history): the step-3 job is being cancelled by something firing between register() and its completion — NOT by my guard, and NOT by a stale jobId in generationService. The store phase-trace will show it. I do NOT think making cancelled->done legal is the fix (agreed — that resurrects the ghost gen).

=== TRACE SNIPPET (for the user to paste at the phantom '1 RUNNING' moment) ===
Have the user open DevTools console and run, the instant the Cue shows the stuck running job:
  copy(JSON.stringify({
    snapshot: window.generationStore?.getSnapshot?.(),
    jobs: window.generationStore?.list?.(),
  }, null, 2))
(If generationStore isn't on window, it's imported — add a temp `window.generationStore = generationStore` in generationStore.js, or read it via the module in Sources. Your call.) Then grep logs/app.log for the stuck job's jobId: look for `illegal transition`, `store.cancel`, a SECOND `register`, or `clearPending` near the step-3 dispatch timestamp.

=== HOUSEKEEPING ===
  • Saw you filed MPI-213 (next_id now 214) — thanks, correct split. My MPI-212 stays scoped to the enqueue guard + reuse image gating only.
  • I'm NOT committing MPI-212 yet (user asked me to hold). When I do, it's generationService.js + promptReuse.js + MpiReusePromptDialog.js + both blocks, by explicit pathspec. None of those touch the store/lane/settle machinery, so no conflict with your MPI-213 fix.
  • The user has the 'Sidecar test' project + PiD installed locally and the app running — fastest path is they paste the trace above. If you'd rather drive it yourself, the project is in their live session; ask them for the folder path.

recent_events.note: Candidate #1 ruled out accepted. Tracing the reuse->dispatch mid-flight cancel path (read-only agent) to find who cancels the active PiD job between register() and completion. Will report the trigger + fix on MPI-213.

recent_events.outcome: MPI-213 lane-key mismatch fixed + live-verified by user; card done. Thread complete.
